BombVault v4 makes your backups **ransomware-resistant** and your restores **transparent**: immutable off-site protection that BombVault actively *verifies* (never just claims), and a restore experience with live progress, a safe cancel, and honest "busy" feedback — hardened by a multi-round adversarial + security review.

## Added

- **Immutable (append-only) off-site.** Flag an off-site repo append-only so ransomware or a compromised host can't delete or rewrite your backups. The far side (a `restic/rest-server` in `--append-only` mode) *enforces* it; BombVault only ever *verifies* it and never shows green on a configuration claim alone.
- **Tamper test.** BombVault periodically proves the append-only guarantee by actually attempting a delete against the off-site repo (aimed at a non-existent object): refused = protected, accepted = not protected. An inconclusive result (server unreachable, auth error, 5xx) never flips the stored verdict, and a real protected → unprotected flip fires a single alert.
- **Guided off-site setup wizard.** Walks you from backend choice (rest-server / rclone / S3) through a ready-to-paste rest-server deploy snippet, a connection test, the immutable toggle (which runs the tamper test immediately), and a retention strategy. Off-site and retention are now first-class (default-mode) features.
- **DR drills from off-site.** Beyond the local integrity drill, BombVault can restore a real target from the *off-site* repo into a throwaway sandbox, verify it file-for-file and byte-for-byte, then clean up — proving you can actually recover from off-site, not just that the repo answers. (VM domains are exempt — their disk images are too large to sandbox-restore.)
- **Ransomware-protection scorecard.** A Dashboard card with a green / amber / red posture per domain and an age-stamped checklist (off-site configured, append-only verified, replication current, restore drill passed, encryption on, prune strategy set); every red row deep-links to the fix. The card and its chip only ever go green on *verified* facts — the per-check states are computed by the backend from the same inputs as the aggregate, so a checklist row can never contradict the chip.
- **Growth-budget alarm** for an immutable off-site (where old snapshots are deliberately never pruned): set a size budget and get alerted before it runs away.
- **Live restore progress.** A long restore now shows a live percentage bar in the restore panel and on the card ("Restoring… NN%"), instead of a bare spinner.
- **Cancel a running restore**, with a type-aware confirmation — a restore-to-a-folder cancels cleanly (the partial output folder is left as-is), while an in-place restore warns that the target is left partially restored. A cancelled restore is recorded distinctly (not as a failure) and fires no failure alert.
- **Honest "busy" feedback.** Starting a backup while a restore (or a scheduled/maintenance operation) holds a repository now returns a clear "a restore is running" message and disables the start buttons with a hint, instead of silently doing nothing.
- Prometheus metrics for the protection posture (immutable flag, tamper-test outcome, last successful replication timestamp), gated to enabled, append-only-claiming domains.

## Changed

- **Off-site path guard.** An off-site path typed without its backend prefix (e.g. `BackBlaze:bucket` instead of `rclone:BackBlaze:bucket`) was silently treated as a *local* folder, so nothing reached the cloud. It is now rejected with guidance to use the correct `rclone:`/`s3:`/`rest:` prefix. (Thanks @BJZwart)
- Off-site **replication currency** now tracks the last *successful* replication, so a silently failing scheduled replication turns the scorecard amber (and notifies) instead of reading green.
- The generated off-site deploy snippet carries a TLS note: plain HTTP is fine on a trusted LAN/VPN, but a WAN-reachable box should terminate TLS so the repository credential isn't sent in the clear.
- The **Security / trust model** section of the README now spells out that the optional password gate is opt-in, and recommends enabling it (and never exposing the port to the internet) once off-site/immutable backups or encryption are in use.
- Numerous review-driven refinements: DR-drill verification tightened to catch a truncated file, tamper verdicts only recorded on decisive HTTP responses, cancelled restores surfaced correctly at the restic process boundary, the destructive Prune kept behind Advanced, added history indexes, restore-folder and DR-drill-target input validation, and full i18n coverage of every new string across all 24 additional languages.

Production installs on `:latest` were unaffected throughout development; v4 was built and box-tested on a `v4-preview` image before this release.

---
